---
title: "Untitled"
author: "Sudeep Santhosh"
date: "2026-03-04"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(haven)
library(labelled)
library(sjlabelled)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
```


loading wave 1 data
```{r}
healthy_wave1=haven::read_sav(here("data","CRONOS3 Theme - Make it Healthy.sav"))
dictionary=labelled::generate_dictionary(healthy_wave1)
```

loading wave 3 data
```{r}
healthy_wave3=haven::read_sav(here("data","CRON3W3e01_1.sav"))
dictionary=labelled::generate_dictionary(healthy_wave3)
```

loading wave 5 data
```{r}
healthy_wave5=haven::read_sav(here("data","CRON3w5e01.sav"))
dictionary_wave_5=labelled::generate_dictionary(healthy_wave5)
```

checking for duplicate values 
```{r}
sum(duplicated(healthy_wave1$idno))
sum(duplicated(healthy_wave5$idno))
sum(duplicated(healthy_wave3$idno))
```

```{r}
healthy_wave1$idno[
  duplicated(healthy_wave1$idno) & healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1 &
  !is.na(healthy_wave1$w1hq3)
]
```
note- in healty wave 1 dataset there were 3 recipants with idno 25213  the same amount was there in healthy wave 3 dataset  
and the same in wave_5 datset


want to see which id of wave_1 are not there in wave 5 
```{r}
setdiff(healthy_wave1$idno[healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1], healthy_wave5$idno)
```
```{r}
setdiff(healthy_wave1$idno[healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1], healthy_wave3$idno)
```
the only common columns in all the data sets is idno country and age 

before removing the values the amount of values who could not see medical specialists
```{r}
sum(healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
```

for now lets proceed with 
removing duplicate values 

```{r}
healthy_wave1=healthy_wave1[!duplicated(healthy_wave1$idno), ]
healthy_wave3=healthy_wave3[!duplicated(healthy_wave3$idno), ]
healthy_wave5=healthy_wave5[!duplicated(healthy_wave5$idno), ]
```

check out the data availability for w1hq3(Needed to see medical specialists (excluding dentists and mental health services) last 12 months)
```{r}
ggplot(healthy_wave1, aes(x = as_factor(w1hq3))) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Needed to See Medical Specialist (Last 12 Months)",
    x = "Response",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
there are 284 repsondants after removing the duplicates we have lost 40 potential data points fo the predictor 
```{r}
sum(healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
```

```{r}
sum(healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1|2|3, na.rm = TRUE)
```

there are around 284 responces which were saying- Yes, but I was unable to do so

284 out of 10716

which is quite normal but lets see the underlying factors

```{r}
length(unique(healthy_wave3$cntry))
```
there are a total of 11 countries out of which  284 people wanted  medical specialists but could not get it 

```{r}
ggplot(healthy_wave1[!is.na(healthy_wave1$w1hq5), ], aes(x = as_factor(w1hq5))) +
  geom_bar() +
  labs(
    title = "Waiting period for a medical specialist",
    x = "Response",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()
```
Less than one month-1
One to two months-2
Three to five month-3
Six to eight months	4
Nine months to one year-5
More than a year-6
I don’t know-7

```{r}
sum((healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1|2|3|4|5|6|7)&(healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1), na.rm = TRUE)
```
just checking if there blank values for this case 


there are some idno number which dont exist in wave 1 dataset

TITLE- Predicting Unmet Need for Medical Specialist Care
 ATTRIBUTE SELECTION 
 dataset name       attribute name
 healthy_wave1      Wait time for medical specialist appointment(w1hq5)
 ''                 Needed to see medical specialists (excluding dentists and mental health services)(w1hq3)
 ''                 Waiting list for diagnostic tests prescribed by a doctor(w1hq13)
 ''                 cntry (country)
 ''                 idno 
                    age
                    
 
 healthy_wave5      Healthcare costs covered by system (w5hq18)
 
 healthy_wave3      Adequate salary considering efforts (w3hq89)
 ''                 Subjective mental health in general(w3hq57)
 ''                 Subjective physical health in general(w3hq58)



analysis in healthywave 5 dataset

not all idno numbers who did not get access to medical specialist have filled the following column Healthcare costs covered by system only 221 out of 284 have filled 
```{r}
sum((healthy_wave1$idno[healthy_wave1$w1hq3==1& !is.na(healthy_wave1$w1hq3)])%in% healthy_wave5$idno)
```
healthy wave 3 dataset 
not all idno numbers who did not get access to medical specialist have filled the following column Healthcare costs covered by system only 237 out of 284 have filled 

```{r}
sum((healthy_wave1$idno[healthy_wave1$w1hq3==1& !is.na(healthy_wave1$w1hq3)])%in% healthy_wave3$idno)
```
```{r}
sum(duplicated(healthy_wave1$idno))
```
```{r}
merged_data=merge(
  healthy_wave1,
  healthy_wave5,
  by = "idno"
)
```

```{r}
merged_data=merge(
  merged_data,
  healthy_wave3,
  by = "idno"
)
```

a lot of loss of data might need to check and with to find a way to tackle the duplicate values 
```{r}
sum(merged_data$w1hq3==1,na.rm = T)
```
 


idea- 2 
Needed to see a general practitioner last 12 months (w1hq1)
Needed to see dentist last 12 months
Needed to see medical specialists (excluding dentists and mental health services) last 12 months
combining all of them then seeing the above idea where we will be seeing in general denied medical health care to get more data points



 no of people who Needed to see a general practitioner last 12 months but could not 
```{r}
sum(healthy_wave1$w1hq1 ==1,na.rm = TRUE)
```
no fo people who Needed to see dentist last 12 months but could not
```{r}
sum(healthy_wave1$w1hq6 ==1,na.rm = TRUE)
```
no of people Needed to see medical specialists but could not 
```{r}
sum(healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1, na.rm = TRUE)
```


```{r}
length(healthy_wave1$idno[
  which(healthy_wave1$w1hq6 == 1|
        healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1|
        healthy_wave1$w1hq1 == 1)
])
```
we have increased the number of observation to 986 rather than just 324

unmet healthcare need
merged Predictor variable required 
dataset           Response variable
healthy_wave1     (w1hq1)   Needed to see a general practitioner last 12 months
healthy_wave1     (w1hq3)   Needed to see medical specialists last 12 months 
healthy_wave1     (w1hq6)   Needed to see dentist last 12 months
healthy_wave1     (w1hq11)  Needed diagnostic tests or scans, prescribed by a doctor last 12

dataset            predicotr variable
healthy_wave1     (w1hq5)   Wait time for medical specialist appointment 
""                (cntry)   Country
""                (age)     age

healthy_wave3     (w3hq77)  Currently in paid work
""                (w3hq57)  Subjective mental health in general
""                (w3hq58)  Subjective physical health in general
""                (w3hq87)  How difficult to take an hour off for personal/family matters during work

healthy_wave5     (w5hq18)  Healthcare costs covered by system
""                (w5hq11)  Receive help and support from close contacts when needed
""                (w5hq8)   Severe financial difficulties in family during first 18 years of life, how often
""                (w5hq17)  Out-of-pocket cost for most recent private mental health consultation
""                (w5hq12)  Felt lonely, how much of the time past week








checking country wise analysis 

filtering out data 
```{r}

healthy_wave1$needed_any <- ifelse(
  healthy_wave1$w1hq1 == 1 |
  healthy_wave1$w1hq3 == 1 |
  healthy_wave1$w1hq6 == 1 |
  healthy_wave1$w1hq11 ==1,
  1, 0
)
```

counting the data
```{r}
healthy_wave1$total_needed <- rowSums(
  healthy_wave1[, c("w1hq1", "w1hq3", "w1hq6","w1hq11")],
  na.rm = TRUE
)
```

```{r}
country_summary=healthy_wave1 %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(
    total_people = n(),
    people_needed = sum(needed_any, na.rm = TRUE),
    proportion = people_needed/total_people
  )
```

```{r}
ggplot(country_summary, aes(x = reorder(cntry, -proportion), 
                            y = proportion)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(
    title = "amount of People Needing Healthcare by Country",
    x = "Country",
    y = "amaount"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
```
Value	Category
AT	Austria
BE	Belgium
CZ	Czechia
FI	Finland
FR	France
GB	United Kingdom
HU	Hungary
IS	Iceland
PL	Poland
PT	Portugal
SI	Slovenia
note there is UK


```{r}
country_summary=healthy_wave1 %>%
  group_by(cntry) %>%
  summarise(
    people_needed = sum(needed_any, na.rm = TRUE),
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(people_needed))

country_summary
```
```{r}
healthy_wave1 |>
  summarise(
    GP=sum(w1hq1==1,na.rm=TRUE),
    Specialist=sum(w1hq3==1,na.rm = TRUE),
    dentist=sum(w1hq6==1,na.rm = TRUE),
    diagnostic_test_req=sum(w1hq11==1,na.rm = TRUE)
  )|>pivot_longer(
    cols=everything(),
    names_to = "Service",
    values_to = "Count"
  )|>
  ggplot(aes(x=Service,y=Count))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity")+
  labs(
    title = " Unmet health care services across 11 countires",
    x = "Service Type",
    y = "Count"
  )+ scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
  GP = "General Practitioner",
  Specialist = "Specialist",
  Dentist = "Dentist",
  diagnostic_test_req = "Diagnostic Test"
))
```

```{r}
healthy_wave1|>
  filter(cntry=="GB")|>
  summarise(
    GP=sum(w1hq1==1,na.rm=TRUE),
    Specialist=sum(w1hq3==1,na.rm = TRUE),
    dentist=sum(w1hq6==1,na.rm = TRUE),
    diagnostic_test_req=sum(w1hq11==1,na.rm = TRUE)
  )|>pivot_longer(
    cols=everything(),
    names_to = "Service",
    values_to = "Count"
  )|>
  ggplot(aes(x=Service,y=Count))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity")+
  labs(
    title = " Unmet health care services United Kingdom",
    x = "Service Type",
    y = "Count"
  )+ scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
  GP = "General Practitioner",
  Specialist = "Specialist",
  Dentist = "Dentist",
  diagnostic_test_req = "Diagnostic Test"
))
```
```{r}
healthy_wave1|>
  filter(cntry=="FI")|>
  summarise(
    GP=sum(w1hq1==1,na.rm=TRUE),
    Specialist=sum(w1hq3==1,na.rm = TRUE),
    dentist=sum(w1hq6==1,na.rm = TRUE),
    diagnostic_test_req=sum(w1hq11==1,na.rm = TRUE)
  )|>pivot_longer(
    cols=everything(),
    names_to = "Service",
    values_to = "Count"
  )|>
  ggplot(aes(x=Service,y=Count))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity")+
  labs(
    title = " Unmet health care services Finland",
    x = "Service Type",
    y = "Count"
  )+ scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
  GP = "General Practitioner",
  Specialist = "Specialist",
  Dentist = "Dentist",
  diagnostic_test_req = "Diagnostic Test"
))
```
```{r}
healthy_wave1|>
  filter(cntry=="BE")|>
  summarise(
    GP=sum(w1hq1==1,na.rm=TRUE),
    Specialist=sum(w1hq3==1,na.rm = TRUE),
    dentist=sum(w1hq6==1,na.rm = TRUE),
    diagnostic_test_req=sum(w1hq11==1,na.rm = TRUE)
  )|>pivot_longer(
    cols=everything(),
    names_to = "Service",
    values_to = "Count"
  )|>
  ggplot(aes(x=Service,y=Count))+
  geom_bar(stat = "identity")+
  labs(
    title = " Unmet health care services Belgium",
    x = "Service Type",
    y = "Count"
  )+ scale_x_discrete(labels = c(
  GP = "General Practitioner",
  Specialist = "Specialist",
  Dentist = "Dentist",
  diagnostic_test_req = "Diagnostic Test"
))
```
there are are a total 
going to see how to handle duplicate values based on 
checking id-25213 
in healthy_wave1 25213 id has two entries with countries as CZ and FI 
trying to see how the ids are filled realized that for every country that a given range of values to assigned 
so as to create unique values we will be merging cntry and idno to create new value which will be the new primary key for filtering 

solving duplicates by merging the cntry and idno column 
checking with healthwave_1 dataset 

```{r}
library(dplyr)

primary_health_wave1=healthy_wave1|>
  mutate(new_id = paste(cntry, idno, sep = "_"))
sum(duplicated(primary_health_wave1$new_id))
```
on merging a column country and idno to create a new unque id there still exist duplicates but has got reduced
